pattern: Since values are bounded, the set of pairwise xors is small, so building it once and xoring it with every distinct element enumerates all reachable triplet values without an O(n^3) scan.
hints: Distinct duplicate values do not create new triplets, so reduce to the set of distinct numbers first.
Compute the set of all pairwise xors; its size is bounded by the value range, not by n squared, once deduplicated.
Xor every pairwise result with every distinct value and collect the outcomes in a set; its size is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: all duplicates collapse to one value
duplicates plus distinct, xor of equal pair yields zero

stress.description: random small array with duplicates, validated by the brute triple oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 1501) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
